name: Maillo - Peña de Francia Trail
name_original: Peña de Francia desde El Maíllo – Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia (PR-SA 7)
description: Hike the challenging 9.7-mile Maillo - Peña de Francia Trail, gaining 2282 feet to panoramic views and a historic sanctuary.
region_intro: Hiking around El Maíllo offers diverse trails within the province of Salamanca, Spain, situated at an elevation of 985 meters (3,232 feet) above sea level. The municipality is an integral part of the Natural Park of Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia and a designated Biosphere Reserve. The landscape features mountainous terrain, extensive oak and pine forests, and holm oak dehesa, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in El Maíllo The most popular hiking route is Laguna de San Marcos – circular via the Camino de Las Raíces - Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Carbonera y Peña Huevo - circular desde La Alberca - Parque Natural de las Batuecas y Sierra de Francia, a difficult 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This trail offers challenging ascents and panoramic views from the peaks. Local hikers also love the Peña de Francia from El Casarito recreational area – Batuecas Natural Park and Sierra de Francia, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain towards the iconic Peña de Francia, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around El Maíllo is defined by mountainous terrain, diverse forest ecosystems including oak and pine, and the unique dehesa landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in El Maíllo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore El Maíllo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Maíllo?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around El Maíllo, offering a wide range of experiences from easy walks to challenging ascents. The area is part of the Natural Park of Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia, providing diverse landscapes for exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near El Maíllo?
A: Hikes near El Maíllo feature diverse terrain, including mountainous areas, extensive oak and pine forests, and holm oak dehesa. You'll find varied environments, from lush valleys to panoramic viewpoints, reflecting the region's status as a Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in El Maíllo?
A: Yes, El Maíllo offers several family-friendly hiking options. There are 6 easy trails suitable for families, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty of the Sierra de Francia at a comfortable pace. The newly established "Los Molinos" route, mentioned in the region research, is designed for accessible exploration with inclusive signage.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around El Maíllo?
A: Many trails in the Natural Park of Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to check specific regulations for each route, especially concerning protected wildlife areas. Keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking?
A: The region boasts several significant landmarks and viewpoints. A highlight is the ascent to Peña de Francia, offering panoramic views from its 1,727-meter summit, where you can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1590368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993027447/laguna-de-san-marcos-circular-via-the-camino-de-las-raices-parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-y-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Marcos – circular via the Camino de Las Raíces&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) trail. Another challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993305574/pena-carbonera-y-pena-huevo-circular-desde-la-alberca-parque-natural-de-las-batuecas-y-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Carbonera y Peña Huevo - circular desde La Alberca&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Maíllo?
A: The best time to hike in El Maíllo is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November) when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher altitudes like Peña de Francia.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in El Maíllo?
A: The trails in El Maíllo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Peña de Francia, and the rich biodiversity found within the Natural Park of Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trekkers, there are 10 difficult trails around El Maíllo. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993039542/pena-de-francia-from-el-casarito-recreational-area-batuecas-natural-park-and-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia from El Casarito recreational area&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993032923/la-alberca-las-batuecas-trail-pr-sa-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alberca - Las Batuecas Trail (PR-SA 10)&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and rewarding views, providing a true test of endurance.

Q: What facilities are available near the hiking areas?
A: Just outside El Maíllo's urban center, you can find the La Hoya recreational area, which offers camping facilities, picnic areas, barbecues, and even a swimming pool and sports courts. Additionally, charming villages like La Alberca, El Cabaco, and Nava de Francia are nearby, providing further amenities and cultural interest.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads?
A: While El Maíllo is accessible by road, public transport options directly to specific trailheads within the Natural Park can be limited. It is generally recommended to use a personal vehicle for maximum flexibility when accessing the more remote starting points for hikes. Check local bus schedules for connections to El Maíllo itself from larger towns in Salamanca province.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or waterfalls?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features. For instance, the region's research mentions the "Chorro de las Batuecas," which implies a waterfall, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497799277/la-alberca-to-chorro-de-las-batuecas-sierra-de-francia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alberca to Chorro de las Batuecas – Sierra de Francia&lt;/a&gt; route leads to this natural attraction. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, offering serene waterside paths.
tips:
Not only does it give its name to the wonderful Natural Park, but its peak at 1,727 meters above sea level offers the best panoramic views in all directions. Right there, at the top, is the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Peña de Francia of the Dominican Order that is worth visiting. And of course, you cannot return without having taken the obligatory photos at the viewpoint and the Balcony of Santiago, two panoramic points par excellence.
You can get here using the means that you like best, either by car, bike or walking, the last modality will give you several alternatives of very picturesque trails from the town of La Alberca or from Camping Sierra de Francia.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Maillo - Peña de Francia Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins relatively flat from the recreational area "La Hoya" in El Maíllo, passing through oak groves and heather. It then enters pine forests, where the ascent gradually steepens with a strong, winding climb. You'll encounter dense oak forests and further steep sections. The final stretch to the summit involves crossing a road, with the terrain becoming more exposed and potentially irregular with loose stones. Proper footwear is highly recommended.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during drier periods. It's advisable to avoid hiking when there's snow or significant water, as lower areas can become muddy and higher elevations can be dangerous. Spring and autumn typically offer more pleasant conditions, but always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the recreational area "La Hoya" in El Maíllo. You should be able to find parking facilities there, as it's the designated starting point for this route.

faqs.question: Is the Maillo - Peña de Francia Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a natural park, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed in the provided information. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash in natural areas to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the potential presence of vipers mentioned in the trail research. Always check local park regulations before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Maillo - Peña de Francia Trail is located within the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park. There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the trail itself or for accessing the natural park. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What notable sights or viewpoints will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: As you ascend, you'll be rewarded with magnificent panoramic views of the "campo charro" of Salamanca. The route also passes by historical elements like the "Casa Baja," an ancient winter residence for the friars. The ultimate destination is the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia, located at the summit, offering expansive vistas from its peak.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a linear route, meaning it is an out-and-back trail. You will retrace your steps to return to the starting point in El Maíllo.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding safety or challenging conditions?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered difficult due to significant elevation gain and steep sections. Some parts may have an irregular surface with loose stones. Be aware of potential extreme conditions with snow and water, which can lead to mud and dangerous icy patches. There is also a potential presence of vipers, so watch your step. Good physical fitness and appropriate gear are essential.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the primary route is Maillo - Peña de Francia (PR-SA 7), sections of this trail overlap with other paths. You might encounter parts of the [CIMA SA06] Peña de Francia * El Cabaco, [CIMA SA06] Peña de Francia * Monsagro, El Cabaco - Peña de Francia, Camino del Convento, and Camino del Santo routes.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for tackling the challenging ascent to Peña de Francia?
faqs.answer: The ascent involves a strong and winding climb, particularly demanding through the dense pine forest and subsequent steep sections. Pace yourself, take breaks as needed, and ensure you carry enough water and snacks. The oak forest sections offer a slight respite before the final push to the summit. Proper hiking boots with good ankle support are crucial for the varied and sometimes irregular terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead or along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the recreational area "La Hoya" in El Maíllo, which may offer basic facilities. The Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia at the summit is a significant site, and typically such locations have some visitor amenities. The "Casa Baja" is a historical structure along the route, but not a modern facility. For more extensive amenities like cafes or restaurants, you would likely need to visit the village of El Maíllo itself.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Peña de Francia
